# Draft

## Draft Body

The review group should approve a two-quarter pilot that separates decision-ready platform review packets from advisory intake. The goal is not to redesign governance. The goal is to stop asking one meeting to both shape early ideas and approve mature decisions.

In the synthetic review packet sample, decision-ready packets moved faster than mixed advisory intake. The sample also suggests that packet completeness improved when teams knew which path they were entering. Those claims should stay scoped to the sample and should not be presented as enterprise-wide proof.

The pilot should use two intake paths. Advisory intake should help teams shape early ideas. Decision review should require a clear ask, options considered, trade-offs, and implementation implications. That separation gives reviewers a cleaner decision surface and gives teams a clearer preparation target.

The current draft still overstates one claim: it says the pilot will reduce avoidable rework. Existing sources make that outcome plausible, but they do not directly prove it. Before revision continues, the paper needs follow-up research that either supports the rework claim, narrows it, or removes it.

The requested decision is to approve the pilot, confirm the two intake paths, and require a research refresh before any exported version claims avoided rework.
